title: Hex Workplan
description: A workplan decomposes a feature into dependency-ordered phases of tasks. Each task maps to one hexagonal adapter boundary. Workplans are stored in docs/workplans/ and executed by HexFlo swarms.

id.description: Unique identifier, prefixed wp-. Example: wp-frontend-hexagonal-architecture

feature.description: Human-readable feature name — displayed in hex plan list

description.description: What this workplan delivers and why

adr.description: ADR reference (ADR-NNN legacy or ADR-YYMMDDHHMM timestamp). Required by ADR-050 pipeline: ADR → Workplan → Swarm

status.description: Workplan lifecycle status. planned=not started, in_progress=active work, done=all phases complete, superseded=replaced by another workplan

priority.description: P0-BLOCKER blocks other work and shows red in hex plan list

blocks.description: What this workplan blocks (shown when priority is P0-BLOCKER)

supersedes.description: ID of workplan this one replaces

supersession_reason.description: Why the old plan was absorbed

phases.description: Execution phases ordered by dependency tier. Phases execute sequentially; tasks within a phase can run in parallel.

description.description: Detailed phase scope and acceptance criteria

gate.description: Build/test gate that must pass before next phase starts

command.description: Shell command to run as gate check

blocking.description: If true, workplan halts on gate failure

tasks.description: Tasks within this phase. Tasks with no inter-deps can run in parallel.

layer.description: Hex architecture layer this task operates in

description.description: Implementation details, acceptance criteria, key decisions

deps.description: Task IDs this task depends on. Empty = can start immediately.

files.description: Files this task will create or modify

agent.description: Agent role to execute this task

strategy_hint.description: Execution strategy hint (ADR-2604131630). scaffold=template codegen, transform=AST transform, script=run command, codegen=code gen (template first, inference fallback), inference=requires LLM reasoning. Absent=auto-classify from task title.
